title: Listening: The Missing Cake
learningGoal: Aurally identify vector verbs indicating completion, self-benefit, and sudden emotional reactions.
instructions: Listen to a frantic argument between roommates over a missing cake. Track exactly what happened to the cake, who arrived, and how people reacted.
listeningGoal: Distinguish between 'ate' and 'ate up', and recognize the suddenness vectors.
audioScenario: Ria comes home to find her birthday cake missing. Amit tries to explain.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: रिया: अमित, मेरा केक कहाँ है? मैं उसे फ्रिज में रख गई थी।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Ria: Amit, mera cake kahan hai? Main use fridge mein rakh gayi thi.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Ria: Amit, where is my cake? I had kept it in the fridge.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: अमित: रिया, मुझे माफ़ कर दो। तुम्हारा भाई आ गया था।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Amit: Ria, mujhe maaf kar do. Tumhara bhai aa gaya tha.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Amit: Ria, please forgive me. Your brother had arrived.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: रिया: तो? क्या उसने मेरा केक खा लिया?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Ria: Toh? Kya usne mera cake kha liya?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Ria: So? Did he eat up my cake?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: अमित: हाँ, वह बहुत भूखा था। उसने सारा केक खा लिया और सारा जूस पी लिया।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Amit: Haan, woh bahut bhookha tha. Usne saara cake kha liya aur saara juice pee liya.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Amit: Yes, he was very hungry. He ate up the whole cake and drank up all the juice.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: रिया: यह सुनकर रिया ज़ोर से रो पड़ी। 'यह मेरा बर्थडे केक था!'
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Ria: Yeh sun kar Ria zor se ro padi. 'Yeh mera birthday cake tha!'
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Ria: Hearing this, Ria suddenly burst into tears. 'This was my birthday cake!'

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: अमित: शांत हो जाओ। मैं तुम्हारे लिए एक नया केक ले आऊँगा।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Amit: Shaant ho jao. Main tumhare liye ek naya cake le aaunga.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Amit: Calm down. I will bring a new cake for you.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: रिया: नहीं! मैं तुम्हारी बात नहीं सुनूँगी।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Ria: Nahi! Main tumhari baat nahi sunungi.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Ria: No! I will not listen to you.

audioTranscriptFull: रिया: अमित, मेरा केक कहाँ है? मैं उसे फ्रिज में रख गई थी। अमित: रिया, मुझे माफ़ कर दो। तुम्हारा भाई आ गया था। रिया: तो? क्या उसने मेरा केक खा लिया? अमित: हाँ, वह बहुत भूखा था। उसने सारा केक खा लिया और सारा जूस पी लिया। रिया: यह सुनकर रिया ज़ोर से रो पड़ी। 'यह मेरा बर्थडे केक था!' अमित: शांत हो जाओ। मैं तुम्हारे लिए एक नया केक ले आऊँगा। रिया: नहीं! मैं तुम्हारी बात नहीं सुनूँगी।

options: She had completely kept it / put it away (Rakh + Jaana).

questionSet.questionText: When Amit says 'Bhai aa gaya tha', what nuance does 'gaya' add?
options: It emphasizes the completed transition of his arrival (He had arrived).
It means the brother went away.
It means the brother is singing.

questionSet.questionText: Why do they say 'Kha liya' and 'Pee liya' instead of just 'Khaya' and 'Piya'?
options: To show that he greedily consumed it for his own benefit (taking the action inward).
Because he fed it to someone else.

questionSet.questionText: What does Ria do after hearing the news?
options: She bursts into tears suddenly (Ro padi).

questionSet.questionText: In her final sentence ('Main tumhari baat nahi sunungi'), why does Ria NOT use a compound verb?
options: Because it is a negative sentence ('nahi').
Because it is in the future tense.

correctAnswers.lq_1: She had completely kept it / put it away (Rakh + Jaana).
correctAnswers.lq_2: It emphasizes the completed transition of his arrival (He had arrived).
correctAnswers.lq_3: To show that he greedily consumed it for his own benefit (taking the action inward).
correctAnswers.lq_4: She bursts into tears suddenly (Ro padi).
correctAnswers.lq_5: Because it is a negative sentence ('nahi').

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent listening! You easily tracked the nuances of who took what, who arrived, and who burst out crying.
feedbackRules.onFail: Listen to the verbs at the end of the sentences: 'gaya', 'liya', 'padi'. These are the vectors.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good attempt. Pay special attention to 'Nahi sunungi' at the end. Notice how there is no vector verb there because it's a negative statement.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: रिया: अमित, मेरा केक कहाँ है? मैं उसे फ्रिज में रख गई थी। अमित: रिया, मुझे माफ़ कर दो। तुम्हारा भाई आ गया था। रिया: तो? क्या उसने मेरा केक खा लिया? अमित: हाँ, वह बहुत भूखा था। उसने सारा केक खा लिया और सारा जूस पी लिया। रिया: यह सुनकर रिया ज़ोर से रो पड़ी। 'यह मेरा बर्थडे केक था!' अमित: शांत हो जाओ। मैं तुम्हारे लिए एक नया केक ले आऊँगा। रिया: नहीं! मैं तुम्हारी बात नहीं सुनूँगी।

hintText: Whenever food or drink is completely consumed, Hindi speakers almost always use 'Lena' (Kha liya, Pee liya) or 'Jaana' (Kha gaya, Pee gaya).
commonMistakes: Assuming 'Aa gaya' means 'came and went'. It just means 'Arrived completely'.
